backend.authStrategy: JWT RS256 access tokens (15 min) + rotating refresh tokens (7 days, HttpOnly cookie)

security.rateLimitingStrategy: Redis sliding window — per-route and global limits

assumptions: Go 1.22 selected for native concurrency, zero-overhead goroutines, and excellent SSE support via Gin's streaming response.
sqlc used for type-safe SQL query generation from .sql files — no ORM, full SQL control, no reflection overhead.
golang-migrate handles schema migrations — SQL-first, CI-friendly, works with raw PostgreSQL.
argon2id via golang.org/x/crypto/argon2 for password hashing (OWASP recommended, pure Go, no CGO).
RS256 JWT signing — private key loaded from JWT_PRIVATE_KEY env var at startup; public key for verification.
User email stored AES-256-GCM encrypted; email_hash (SHA-256) used for login lookup.
Redis pub/sub fan-out for SSE — all Fly.io regions subscribe to same channel; no sticky sessions needed.
Tigris (Fly.io's S3-compatible storage) for file attachments — presigned PUT URLs, backend never buffers files.
go-redis/v9 client with connection pooling (max 20); gobreaker circuit breaker on rate-limit Redis calls.
retry-go library for Resend email delivery: 3 attempts, exponential backoff + jitter, 10s per-attempt timeout.
Fly.io fly.toml declares primary PostgreSQL region (iad); read replicas auto-provisioned in secondary regions.
CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var (comma-separated) loaded at startup — wildcard origin not permitted.
GitHub Actions CI: go vet → staticcheck → gosec → test (Postgres service container) → build → fly deploy.
Prometheus /metrics endpoint exposed on internal port only — not publicly accessible.
